embeds_html:
Nano G2 Ultra
The Nano G2 Ultra features the low power MCU NRF52840 and the internal wideband Lora antenna, which supports frequencies from 815 Mhz to 940 Mhz. It also takes into account the potential effect of the human body on its antenna performance.

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: I flashed back to @ded (SalishMesh) 's regular FS build. These are the steps I took in this order: unplugged USB cable confirm GPS physical switch is still at the on position power cycled device off and on connect USB cable, start Putty to connect to serial output confirm Position setting is disabled because of power cycle enable position in app, tap checkmark to save confirm the GPS PPS LED on the left side of the device is pulsing Continue to get zero sats
messages.content_html:
I flashed back to @ded (SalishMesh)'s regular FS build. These are the steps I took in this order:
  • unplugged USB cable
  • confirm GPS physical switch is still at the on position
  • power cycled device off and on
  • connect USB cable, start Putty to connect to serial output
  • confirm Position setting is disabled because of power cycle
  • enable position in app, tap checkmark to save
  • confirm the GPS PPS LED on the left side of the device is pulsing
Continue to get zero sats
